title: podalyria aurea
intro: Podalyria aurea, also known as the Golden Cassia or Golden Bushwillow, is a shrub or small tree native to southern Africa. Traditionally, the plant's bark and leaves have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Podalyria aurea include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety.
